expectations.text: Declares an email/notifier interface (port) in the reason — app/ — owned where it's needed
expectations.evidence: New app/users/notifications.py declaring WelcomeNotifier Protocol, mirroring repository.py

expectations.text: Places the SendGrid SDK implementation in the connections (infra/), not in app/
expectations.evidence: infra/sendgrid/welcome_notifier.py holds SendGridWelcomeNotifier

expectations.text: Keeps the SendGrid SDK import out of app/ — the reason takes on no outward runtime dependency on SendGrid
expectations.evidence: Explicit: 'No SendGrid types, no API keys, no import sendgrid anywhere in app/'

expectations.text: Wires the concrete SendGrid implementation in run/main.py (how it's run)
expectations.evidence: build_signup() constructs notifier from env and injects into SignUp

expectations.text: Has the signup use-case invoke the email step through the interface — the decision to send the welcome email lives in the reason
expectations.evidence: SignUp.__call__ calls self._notifier.send_welcome(user) after save; 'welcome on signup is a business rule'

expectations.text: Identifies that database/sql I/O inside the domain Order type is a smell — the reason points outward at a concrete
expectations.evidence: 'I/O and an outside concrete living in the reason'; 'dependency arrow points the wrong way'

expectations.text: Recommends a repository-style interface (port) owned by the domain/reason
expectations.evidence: Repository interface declared in internal/order, 'the reason owns this interface'

expectations.text: Moves the SQL into an adapter/connection (e.g. internal/postgres), leaving the domain free of database/sql
expectations.evidence: internal/order/postgres/repository.go holds the INSERT; drop database/sql from order.go

expectations.text: Keeps the order-total business rule in the domain, not pushed into the adapter
expectations.evidence: Place() retains the total computation and empty-order check, no I/O

expectations.text: Notes the construction/wiring belongs in cmd (how it's run)
expectations.evidence: cmd/server/main.go opens DB, builds postgres repo, wires service

expectations.text: Reuses the existing reindexCatalog use-case in core rather than duplicating its logic
expectations.evidence: 'imports the same reindexCatalog'; 'the reindex logic itself does not move'

expectations.text: Adds the new CLI as an entrypoint under run/ (how it's run)
expectations.evidence: New src/run/reindexCli.ts alongside server.ts

expectations.text: Wires the same adapters (ProductStore, SearchIndex) into the CLI entrypoint
expectations.evidence: Constructs PostgresProductStore + ElasticSearchIndex, same as server

expectations.text: Keeps the CLI entrypoint thin — no business logic added to run/
expectations.evidence: 'Don't put reindex logic in reindexCli.ts; only construct, call, and report'

expectations.text: Does not introduce a new outward dependency from core to make this work
expectations.evidence: 'core gains a second driver without learning anything about HTTP or cron'; core untouched

expectations.text: Declares an email/notifier interface (port) in the reason — app/ — owned where it's needed
expectations.evidence: New app/users/notifications.py with WelcomeMailer Protocol, by analogy with repository.py

expectations.text: Places the SendGrid SDK implementation in the connections (infra/), not in app/
expectations.evidence: infra/sendgrid/welcome_mailer.py SendGridWelcomeMailer

expectations.text: Keeps the SendGrid SDK import out of app/ — the reason takes on no outward runtime dependency on SendGrid
expectations.evidence: 'If you ever find yourself writing import sendgrid inside app/, the email code has landed in the wrong layer'

expectations.text: Wires the concrete SendGrid implementation in run/main.py (how it's run)
expectations.evidence: build_signup() builds SendGridAPIClient from env and injects mailer

expectations.text: Has the signup use-case invoke the email step through the interface — the decision to send the welcome email lives in the reason
expectations.evidence: SignUp calls self._mailer.send_welcome(user); 'policy decision lives in the use-case'

expectations.text: Identifies that database/sql I/O inside the domain Order type is a smell — the reason points outward at a concrete
expectations.evidence: 'dependency-direction inversion'; 'business core depends on infrastructure'

expectations.text: Recommends a repository-style interface (port) owned by the domain/reason
expectations.evidence: Repository interface in internal/order/repository.go (the port)

expectations.text: Moves the SQL into an adapter/connection (e.g. internal/postgres), leaving the domain free of database/sql
expectations.evidence: internal/order/postgres/repository.go with compile-time var _ order.Repository check

expectations.text: Keeps the order-total business rule in the domain, not pushed into the adapter
expectations.evidence: Place() stays pure: validate, sum Total, set Status

expectations.text: Notes the construction/wiring belongs in cmd (how it's run)
expectations.evidence: main is the composition root: opens db, wires repo -> service

expectations.text: Reuses the existing reindexCatalog use-case in core rather than duplicating its logic
expectations.evidence: 'Both entrypoints call it. The logic lives in one place and is invoked twice'

expectations.text: Adds the new CLI as an entrypoint under run/ (how it's run)
expectations.evidence: New src/run/reindex-cli.ts next to server.ts

expectations.text: Wires the same adapters (ProductStore, SearchIndex) into the CLI entrypoint
expectations.evidence: Reuses ElasticSearchIndex + PostgresProductStore + pool verbatim

expectations.text: Keeps the CLI entrypoint thin — no business logic added to run/
expectations.evidence: 'no new logic'; only main(), log line, exit code added

expectations.text: Does not introduce a new outward dependency from core to make this work
expectations.evidence: core/ports.ts and reindexCatalog.ts untouched; 'No new port is needed'
